name: Monorepo-Bounced Tool Calls
rule: Every mutating, architectural, schema-dependent, deployment, GitHub, database, browser-QA, authentication, or external-service action must pass through ToolGateway. The gateway hydrates AGENTS.md, operator_invariants.json, monorepo_manifest.yaml, truth_surfaces.yaml, relevant tool_contracts, Beads, Memory views, and existing package implementations before allowing execution.

name: Two-Lane Upload Doctrine
rule: Git is for canonical source, small docs, manifests, build files, tests, scripts, and policy. Large binaries, raw archives, external mirrors, generated indexes, vector DBs, notebook outputs, videos, model files, and build artifacts go to artifact storage or ignored local mirrors. Commit manifests, not payloads.

name: GitHub App Push Gate
rule: Automated pushes must use short-lived GitHub App installation tokens through scripts/auth_github_app.py or an approved wrapper. Token renewal handles expiration only; it is not a bypass for file-size limits, LFS budget, branch protection, or secret scanning.

name: No Markdown Plan Sprawl
rule: Long-horizon work belongs in Beads. Durable knowledge belongs in Memory atoms. Research synthesis belongs in the knowledge vault. Evidence belongs in .agent/evidence.

name: Research Wiki Is Synthesis, Not Source Truth
rule: LLM-compiled Markdown wikis are allowed for research synthesis, Q&A, reports, slides, and concept mapping. They must not override Git source, Bazel build truth, GitNexus/SCIP code truth, Beads task truth, Memory atom operational truth, or ToolGateway contracts.

name: Gate 0 UI Discipline
rule: Frontend changes must pass Gate 0: Biome, design-token lint, Taste profile review, and screenshot/video proof where applicable. Raw design values are forbidden outside token/spec files unless explicitly approved.

name: Bootstrap Alignment Is Phased Work
rule: Bootstrap sequences that touch auth, workspace settings, indexes, repo clones, ZIP payloads, lint fixes, or validation must be decomposed into Beads issues and executed through ToolGateway contracts. They must not run as one broad bash payload.

name: Taste Is A Gate, Not A Vibe
rule: Taste Skill concepts may guide design output, but repo-owned design.md, tokens.css, screenshots, accessibility checks, and lint gates remain the source of truth.

name: Autolint Scope Boundary
rule: Autolint daemons may perform deterministic formatting and lint autofixes only. They may not perform broad refactors, archive deletion, history rewrites, dependency migrations, IDE hot-swaps, or direct production-affecting changes. Non-lint architectural changes require a separate branch, GitNexus impact check, build/test evidence, and human review.

name: Artifact Manifest Requirement
rule: Any non-source payload moved out of Git must be represented by an artifact manifest containing URI, sha256, owner, retention class, source, status, and retrieval instructions. The manifest is committed; the payload is not.

name: History Bloat Requires History Tools
rule: Git history rewrites (filter-branch, filter-repo, BFG) are the ONLY authorized method for removing accidentally committed large objects from history. Force-pushing rewritten history requires STATE B approval. Objects already in pack history cannot be removed by gitignore alone.

name: Secret Scanner Succession
rule: Betterleaks is the canonical secret scanner. Gitleaks is the legacy fallback. Both must share the same .gitleaksignore suppression file. Scanner upgrades must maintain fingerprint compatibility. CI gates must never regress — a secret that was blocked must remain blocked after scanner migration.

name: External Repos Are Reference Mirrors
rule: Repositories cloned into external_repos/ are read-only reference mirrors governed by external_repos/upstream_manifest.yaml. They must never be modified locally, committed to the monorepo history, or treated as canonical source. The manifest tracks origin, branch, shallow depth, group, and purpose. Clone operations use scripts/clone-external-reference-repos.sh exclusively.

name: SkillOps Is Repo-Governed
rule: Skill creation, update, archival, and deduplication must flow through the SkillOps subsystem: tool_contracts/skills.update.yaml defines the contract, scripts/skills-audit.sh validates fleet integrity, scripts/skills-registry.py manages the registry, and .ruler/skills.md propagates rules to agents. Ad-hoc skill file manipulation without registry update is prohibited.

name: Boot Probes Are Read-Only
rule: Session boot probes (MCP pre-flight, health checks, environment reads, manifest parsing, heartbeat queries) must be strictly read-only. They must not mutate files, create branches, push commits, modify configuration, or trigger side effects. Boot failures must be reported, not auto-remediated without explicit operator approval.
